verses.text: 'Let every soul be subject unto the higher powers... the powers that be are ordained of God.' Ordained order. Civil rule is God's appointment.
'Let every soul be subject unto the higher powers... the powers that be are ordained of God.' Ordained order. Civil rule is God's appointment.
Rulers are 'a terror' to evil, not good; 'he is the minister of God to thee for good.' For our good. Government serves God's purpose of order.
Rulers are 'a terror' to evil, not good; 'he is the minister of God to thee for good.' For our good. Government serves God's purpose of order.
Be subject for conscience' sake; 'render therefore to all their dues,' tribute and honour. Pay your dues. The Christian owes the state its honour and tax.
Be subject for conscience' sake; 'render therefore to all their dues,' tribute and honour. Pay your dues. The Christian owes the state its honour and tax.
Be subject for conscience' sake; 'render therefore to all their dues,' tribute and honour. Pay your dues. The Christian owes the state its honour and tax.
Owe no man anything but love; 'love is the fulfilling of the law.' Love sums all. To love truly is to keep the whole law.
Owe no man anything but love; 'love is the fulfilling of the law.' Love sums all. To love truly is to keep the whole law.
Owe no man anything but love; 'love is the fulfilling of the law.' Love sums all. To love truly is to keep the whole law.
It is 'high time to awake'; the night is far spent; 'put on the armour of light.' Wake up. The nearness of day calls for holy living.
It is 'high time to awake'; the night is far spent; 'put on the armour of light.' Wake up. The nearness of day calls for holy living.
Walk honestly, 'put ye on the Lord Jesus Christ,' and make no provision for the flesh. Clothed with Christ. Put on the Saviour, not the works of darkness.
